hypothesis: Baseline: GLV brand voice rules applied as written. Hypothesis: strict adherence to blog-config.md banned phrase list + Canadian English + short paragraphs produces drafts that need minimal revision from Aiden.

Opening paragraphs that lead with a locally-specific seasonal moment or Northern Ontario reader identity statement — before naming the product — will score higher than product-first openings. Evidence: baseline drafts were technically correct but hooks were generic. Hypothesis: 'First boat of the season on St. Marys River.' or 'Sault Ste. Marie gets 290 cm of snow a year.' pulls the reader into their own world first, then delivers the product as the solution. Target: 8+/10.

Exploit the identity-first hook pattern but add a single verifiable local fact as the lede sentence. Evidence: exp_1776810532 kept at 8/10 — identity-first hooks work, but the evaluator noted 'could be even more specific — a single verifiable local fact (snowfall figure, ice-out date, water depth) would sharpen further.' Hypothesis: opening with one concrete, verifiable Northern Ontario fact (e.g. 'Sault Ste. Marie averages 290 cm of snow a year.' / 'The St. Marys River drops 6.4 metres over 1.5 km.' / 'Ice goes out on Lake Superior in early May most years.') before the identity statement will score 9/10 by creating immediate reader credibility and local recognition. Constraint: fact must be publicly verifiable — no invented stats.

Tighten the verifiable-fact lede constraint: lede must be Northern Ontario-specific (St. Marys River, Lake Superior, Algoma district geography/weather/season) — not general industry statistics. Evidence: exp_1777002297 kept at 9/10 but blogs 3 and 4 used industry facts (50-hour oil change interval, spark plug failure rate) rather than local facts — these ledes are noticeably weaker than the Lake Superior ice-out and walleye season openers. Hypothesis: enforcing the Northern Ontario-specific requirement on every lede will produce consistent 9-10/10 drafts with no weak generic openers. Secondary change: vary lede sentence structure (measurement, date, geographic scale, seasonal event) so patterns don't feel formulaic across a batch.

Adding explicit body structure rule — CMI 4-stage outline (reader pain → product as solution → proof via specifics → seasonal/urgency CTA) combined with Copyblogger objection principle (answer the most common buyer hesitation with a real number, timeline, or certification before the CTA) — will push consistent draft quality from 9 to 10/10 by engineering the body with the same precision as the lede. Evidence: exp_3 kept at 9 with lede engineered; scout signals (Apr 29) confirmed CMI framework + Copyblogger objection FAQs as the next structural lever. Lede gate already validated — body is the remaining gap.
